Package org.apache.juneau.rest.client

Class RestResponse

java.lang.Object
org.apache.juneau.rest.client.RestResponse
All Implemented Interfaces:
Closeable, AutoCloseable
public final class RestResponse extends Object implements Closeable
An HTTP response returned by RestRequest.run().

Wraps the transport-layer TransportResponse and provides higher-level accessors. Callers must close this response (it implements Closeable) to release transport resources.

Beta — API subject to change: This type is part of the next-generation REST client and HTTP stack (org.apache.juneau.marshall.ng.*). It is not API-frozen: binary- and source-incompatible changes may appear in the next major Juneau release (and possibly earlier).

Since:
9.2.1

  • Method Details

    • getClient

      public RestClient getClient()
      Returns the client that produced this response.

      Beta — API subject to change: This type is part of the next-generation REST client and HTTP stack (org.apache.juneau.marshall.ng.*). It is not API-frozen: binary- and source-incompatible changes may appear in the next major Juneau release (and possibly earlier).

      Returns:
      The client that produced this response. Never null.

    • getStatusCode

      public int getStatusCode()
      Returns the HTTP status code.
      Returns:
      The status code (e.g. 200).

    • getReasonPhrase

      public String getReasonPhrase()
      Returns the reason phrase (e.g. "OK"), or null if absent.
      Returns:
      The reason phrase, possibly null.

    • getHeaders

      public List<TransportHeader> getHeaders()
      Returns all response headers.
      Returns:
      An unmodifiable list. Never null.

    • getFirstHeader

      public TransportHeader getFirstHeader(String name)
      Returns the first response header with the given name (case-insensitive), or null if absent.
      Parameters:
      name - The header name. Must not be null.
      Returns:
      The first matching header, or null.

    • getBodyAsString

      public String getBodyAsString() throws IOException
      Returns the response body as a UTF-8 string, or null if there is no body.
      Returns:
      The body as a string, possibly null.
      Throws:
      IOException - If an I/O error occurs reading the body.

    • getBodyStream

      public InputStream getBodyStream()
      Returns the raw response body stream, or null if there is no body.

      Callers must not close this stream directly — close the RestResponse instead.

      Returns:
      The body stream, possibly null.

    • assertOk

      public RestResponse assertOk() throws RestCallException
      Asserts that the status code is in the 2xx range.
      Returns:
      This object (for chaining).
      Throws:
      RestCallException - If the status code is not 2xx.

    • assertStatus

      public RestResponse assertStatus(int expected) throws RestCallException
      Asserts that the status code equals the expected value.
      Parameters:
      expected - The expected status code.
      Returns:
      This object (for chaining).
      Throws:
      RestCallException - If the status code does not match.

    • body

      public ResponseBody body()
      Returns a fluent body accessor for this response.
      Returns:
      A new body accessor. Never null.

    • assertThat

      public ResponseAssertion assertThat()
      Returns a fluent assertion object for this response.

      Use this for test-style validation of the response in production or test code:

      resp.assertThat() .statusCode(200) .body().contains("alice");

      Returns:
      A new assertion object. Never null.

    • header

      public ResponseHeader header(String name)
      Returns a fluent header accessor for the named response header.
      Parameters:
      name - The header name (case-insensitive). Must not be null.
      Returns:
      A new header accessor. Never null.

    • close

      public void close() throws IOException
      Specified by:
      close in interface AutoCloseable
      Specified by:
      close in interface Closeable
      Throws:
      IOException
